compiled_state.title: Apply TOON format optimization to ticket system output and verbose script results
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
Apply Token-Oriented Object Notation (TOON) encoding to structured data outputs that are consumed by LLM agents, reducing token usage by 30-60% on uniform tabular data.

## What is TOON

TOON (Token-Oriented Object Notation) is a compact serialization format that declares field names once as a header, then emits value-only rows. It is lossless — round-trips back to identical JSON. Benchmarked at 76.4% accuracy vs. JSON's 70.7% on retrieval tasks, with ~52% token reduction on uniform arrays.

Example — ticket list (before, JSON):
\`\`\`json
[{"ticket_id": "abc-1234", "ticket_type": "bug", "title": "Auth fails", "status": "open", "priority": 2}, {"ticket_id": "def-5678", "ticket_type": "epic", "title": "Add login", "status": "open", "priority": 1}]
\`\`\`

After (TOON):
\`\`\`
[2]{ticket_id,ticket_type,title,status,priority}:
  abc-1234,bug,Auth fails,open,2
  def-5678,epic,Add login,open,1
\`\`\`

## Where to Apply

### 1. Ticket System Output
- \`ticket list\` — the primary candidate; returns uniform arrays of ticket objects with identical schemas. At scale (50+ tickets), JSON field name repetition wastes significant tokens.
- \`ticket deps\` — dependency graph output is tabular.
- \`ticket show\` — individual ticket display; less benefit (single object), but comments array could use TOON.
- Add a \`--format=toon\` flag to ticket CLI commands. Default remains JSON for backward compatibility; agents opt into TOON when injecting into prompts.

### 2. Test Results
- \`validate.sh --ci\` output — test pass/fail results are uniform arrays (test name, status, duration).
- \`test-batched.sh\` output — per-test results with runner, status, exit code.
- \`record-test-status.sh\` — test gate status records.
- Test results passed to sub-agents during TEST-FAILURE-DISPATCH are prime candidates — the sub-agent receives a list of failures with identical schema.

### 3. Script Outputs Consumed by Agents
- \`sprint-list-epics.sh\` — epic listing with uniform fields.
- \`review-complexity-classifier.sh\` — scoring output.
- \`issue-quality-check.sh\` — quality scores per ticket.
- \`enrich-file-impact.sh\` — file impact arrays.
- Any script whose output is interpolated into a sub-agent dispatch prompt.

## Implementation Approach

1. Add a TOON encoder utility (Python, minimal — the format is simple enough for a ~50-line function)
2. Add \`--format=toon|json\` flag to ticket CLI and key scripts
3. Update sub-agent dispatch prompts in sprint/debug-everything to request TOON format when passing structured data
4. Measure token savings on real ticket lists and test outputs to validate the 30-60% range for our data shapes
5. Do NOT apply TOON to prose/narrative content (descriptions, comments) — savings are negligible (<10%) on unstructured text

## What NOT to TOON-encode
- Skill instruction text (SKILL.md) — prose, not tabular
- CLAUDE.md content — prose
- Ticket descriptions and comments — unstructured text
- Error messages — human-readable prose
- Any output meant for human consumption (TOON is for agent-to-agent data transfer)

Reference: https://github.com/toon-format/spec (MIT, v3.0)
